name: La Najarra and Pinares de Canencia Loop
name_original: Puerto de Canencia desde Miraflores de la Sierra – circular por camino La Najarra
description: This difficult 31.5-mile mountain biking loop in ZEPA Alto Lozoya offers challenging terrain, pine forests, and mountain views.
region_intro: Miraflores De La Sierra mountain bike trails are situated within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park and the Regional Park of the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares, offering diverse terrain for cyclists. The region is characterized by its valley location, framed by massifs of the Central System, including demanding climbs to summits like La Najarra and the dramatic backdrop of the Cuerda Larga. Mountain bikers can explore extensive pine and oak forests, picturesque valleys such as the Hueco de San Blas, and river areas like the Guadalix. This varied landscape provides a mix of cross-country, enduro, and gravel biking opportunities. Best mountain bike trails around Miraflores De La Sierra The most popular mountain bike trail is Hoya de San Blas, Morcuera, Canencia — Sierra de Guadarrama, a 30.5 miles (49.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 12 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes demanding climbs and panoramic views from mountain passes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pinares de Canencia circular hike from Miraflores de la Sierra — Guadarrama National Park, a moderate 19.9 miles (32.0 km) path. This route winds through the scenic pine forests of Canencia within the Guadarrama National Park. Local mountain bikers also love the Paseo por el hueco de S. Blas — Sierra de Guadarrama, a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail leading through the well-preserved Hueco de San Blas valley, often completed in about 1 hour 11 minutes. Mountain biking around Miraflores De La Sierra is defined by mountain passes like La Morcuera, extensive pine and oak forests, and picturesque valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to challenging ascents. The routes in Miraflores De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 1,400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Miraflores De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: Miraflores De La Sierra offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 40 routes available for exploration. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy paths to challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: The region boasts diverse terrain, including extensive pine and oak forests, picturesque valleys like the Hueco de San Blas, and river areas. You'll find a mix of cross-country, enduro, and gravel biking opportunities, with paths winding through wooded areas and up mountain passes like La Morcuera.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: Yes, Miraflores De La Sierra has several routes suitable for beginners. There are 3 easy mountain bike trails that offer a gentler introduction to the area's beautiful landscapes.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 19 difficult mountain bike trails. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1338568152/hoya-de-san-blas-morcuera-canencia-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoya de San Blas, Morcuera, Canencia — Sierra de Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt; feature demanding climbs and panoramic views from mountain passes.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: Yes, many routes in Miraflores De La Sierra are circular. An excellent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197067372/pinares-de-canencia-circular-hike-from-miraflores-de-la-sierra-guadarrama-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinares de Canencia circular hike from Miraflores de la Sierra — Guadarrama National Park&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through scenic pine forests.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while mountain biking in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore shady Monte Aguirre pine forests, vibrant oak groves, and picturesque valleys. The area also features cold, crystal-clear springs and rivers like the Guadalix, which forms the double &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, scattered throughout the natural landscape are vestiges of past human activity, such as sheepfolds, shepherd huts, old charcoal kilns, and ancient dry stone walls. You might also encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2555399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/434252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares el Real Castle&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible from the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area is home to several beautiful waterfalls. The most notable is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, formed by the Guadalix river. Other waterfalls in the vicinity include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/851377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Purgatory Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2685066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo de la Vejiga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Miraflores De La Sierra?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 50 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural scenery, and the extensive network of trails that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Is it possible to reach the mountain bike trails using public transport?
A: Miraflores de la Sierra is well-connected, being just 49 kilometers from Madrid. While specific public transport options to trailheads vary, the town itself is accessible, making it a good base for exploring the surrounding trails.

Q: Are there any specific places to explore for scenic views?
A: Yes, the region offers numerous spots for impressive panoramic views. Mountain passes like La Morcuera are well-known for their vistas of the Sierra. The snowy peaks of the Cuerda Larga also provide a dramatic backdrop, especially in winter.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or lakes?
A: Yes, the Miraflores de la Sierra reservoir provides scenic spots, and some routes may pass by it. Additionally, nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/484448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrezuela Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering beautiful waterside views.

The port of Canencia is one of the most pleasant to climb in the Sierra de Guadarrama. On any of its slopes, both from Miraflores de la Sierra and from the town of Canencia, the slopes are not too steep and the landscape is incredible.

The Church of the Immaculate Conception is, without a doubt, from a historical and artistic point of view, the most outstanding building in Bustarviejo's cultural heritage.

The comprehensive restoration work carried out on the church in Bustarviejo between 2006 and 2009, financed by the Community of Madrid, reveals the great antiquity of this cultural landmark. Architecturally, the interior has a Latin cross floor plan and three naves separated by semicircular arches, with a large triumphal arch separating the transept from the central nave. This arch rests on the church's only column and a large rectangular pillar. The rear façade features a large apse that projects from the rest of the building.

The tower, dating from the late 15th or early 16th century and considered the oldest element of the building, has a square floor plan. It is constructed of irregular masonry with ashlar at the corners and rests on a masonry base. The atrium features a series of seven smooth-shafted columns joined by a wooden architrave built in the early 17th century (1600-1617).

The archaeological excavation revealed a medieval rock-cut necropolis with rectangular and ovoid pits carved into the rock and covered with granite slabs, as well as an anthropomorphic sarcophagus. In addition, the remains of medieval mural paintings were discovered, of great interest due to their subject matter and age (14th century).

More at https://bigjoe.es/ruta-mtb-guadalix-navalafuente-bustarviejo-miraflores/

faqs.question: How difficult is this mountain biking loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as difficult. While some sections in Pinares de Canencia can be easy, the overall route includes challenging mountainous sections requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills. Be prepared for varied terrain, including forest tracks and more demanding paths, some of which might even require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking loop?
faqs.answer: The La Najarra and Pinares de Canencia Loop is approximately 50.75 km long and typically takes around 5 hours and 10 minutes to complete by mountain bike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse range of terrain. The route winds through magnificent Scots pine forests, alongside streams, and across high mountain pastures. While some parts may be firm track, other sections will be more technical and challenging, characteristic of the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains.

faqs.question: What natural features and viewpoints can be seen along the way?
faqs.answer: The loop offers stunning natural beauty. You'll encounter the Crossing of the Morcuera Stream, the Waterfall of the Arroyo de la Vejiga, and the Arroyo de las Chorreras. From higher points, such as the Altos de La Morcuera, you'll enjoy extensive views of the Hontanar peaks, the Canencia valley, and the Peñalara and La Najarra massifs. The Canencia Pass is another notable feature.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: The scenery is beautiful year-round. Spring brings full waterfalls, and autumn displays vibrant foliage. However, winter can be challenging due to frozen cascades and ice. For the most comfortable riding conditions, spring through early autumn is generally recommended.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the La Najarra and Pinares de Canencia Loop?
faqs.answer: Miraflores de la Sierra is a common starting point and a cycling hub in the Madrid community, offering access to the Canencia and Morcuera mountain passes. You can typically find parking in or around this town, which is close to the La Najarra area.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to mountain bike this route, especially within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park or ZEPA Alto Lozoya?
faqs.answer: This route is located within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park and the ZEPA Alto Lozoya. Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required for mountain biking on established trails within these areas. However, it's always advisable to check the official park regulations for any updates or specific restrictions, especially concerning sensitive ecological zones or seasonal closures.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park generally allows dogs, they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always follow local signage and regulations regarding pets.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails. You will ride along parts of the Pinares de Canencia, La Najarra, Camino de Santiago Complutense, El Hueco de San Blas, and the Centro BTT "Valle del Lozoya" - Ruta 5, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to refuel near the trail?
faqs.answer: Miraflores de la Sierra, a town often used as a starting point, offers various facilities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options where you can refuel before or after your ride. The Church of the Immaculate Conception, Bustarviejo is also along the route, with Bustarviejo being another town with amenities.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation gain, this specific loop is generally not recommended for families or beginners. While some individual trails within Pinares de Canencia are easy and family-friendly, this combined loop requires good fitness and advanced mountain biking skills.
